title: David Boyle (rugby league, born 1971)
wikitext:
{{Short description|Australian rugby league footballer}} {{for|the former South Sydney Rabbitohs player|David Boyle (rugby league, born 1959)}} {{Use dmy dates|date=June 2020}} {{Use Australian English|date=July 2013}} {{Infobox rugby league biography |name = David Boyle |fullname = |image = |caption = |birth_date = {{Birth date and age| 1971|07|03|df=yes}} |birth_place = [[Bega, New South Wales|Bega]], [[New South Wales]], [[Australia]] |height = {{convert|180|cm|ftin|abbr=on}} |weight = {{convert|93|kg|stlb|abbr=on}} |retired = yes |position = {{Rlp|CE|WG}} |club1 = [[Canberra Raiders]] |year1start = 1991 |year1end = 98 |appearances1 = 117 |tries1 = 35 |goals1 = 2 |fieldgoals1 = 0 |points1 = 144 |club2 = [[Bradford Bulls]] |year2start = 1999 |year2end = 00 |appearances2 = 56 |tries2 = 17 |goals2 = 0 |fieldgoals2 = 1 |points2 = 69 |source =[http://www.rugbyleagueproject.com/players/David-Boyle-cn-/summary.html Rugby League Project][http://www.yesterdayshero.com.au/PlayerProfile_Boyle-Boyle_11236.aspx Yesterday's Hero] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080724023719/http://www.yesterdayshero.com.au/PlayerProfile_David-Boyle_11236.aspx |date=24 July 2008 }} | module = {{infobox person | embed = yes | father = | mother = | spouse = | education = | alma_mater = | relatives = [[Morgan Boyle]] (son)
[[Millie Boyle]] (daughter)
[[Jason Croker]] (brother-in-law)
[[Lachlan Croker]] (nephew) }} }} '''David Boyle''' (born 3 July 1971{{cite book| editor=Gary Lester |title=The Sun Book of Rugby League – 1983|year=1983|publisher=John Fairfax Marketing|location=Sydney|isbn=0-909558-83-3|pages=78}}) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. He played for the [[Canberra Raiders]] in the [[New South Wales Rugby League]] (NSWRL) competition and for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]]. Boyle played in the [[Rugby league positions#Backs|backs]], chiefly at {{rlp|ce}}. He is the father of [[Morgan Boyle]] and [[Millie Boyle]].{{cite web|author=1 hr ago |url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/rugby-league/canberra-raiders/raiders-juniors-morgan-boyle-to-keep-footy-in-the-family-20131129-2yg1m.html |title=Raiders juniors' Morgan Boyle to keep footy in the family |publisher=Canberratimes.com.au |date=29 November 2013 |accessdate=8 April 2017}} ==Playing career== ===Canberra=== Boyle was graded by the Raiders during the 1990 season. He made his first grade debut from the bench in his sides' 20–18 win over the [[Gold Coast Chargers|Gold Coast Seagulls]] at [[Bruce Stadium]] in round 4 of the 1991 season. Boyle also scored his first try in his debut match. He was one of the Raiders' shining prospects when the club was forced to release several star players due to salary cap problems at the end of the 1991 season. Most of the Raiders players (including Boyle himself) agreed to take a pay cut to keep the side together. The Raiders missed the finals for the first time since 1986 when they finished twelfth in [[1992 NSWRL season|the 1992 season]].{{cite book|last=Middleton|first=David|title=Rugby League 1992/1993|page=49|isbn=1-875471-23-5}} The Raiders came back strongly in 1993, with their international stars [[Mal Meninga]], [[Ricky Stuart]], [[Laurie Daley]], [[Bradley Clyde]] and [[Steve Walters]], alongside tryscoring Fijian sensation [[Noa Nadruku]] (22 tries for the season) leading the way. Canberra finished third after the regular season, and were premiership favorites until their fateful round 21 match with the hapless [[Parramatta Eels|Parramatta]] at Bruce Stadium. Halfback Ricky Stuart badly broke and dislocated his right ankle in the second half and despite a club record 68–0 win, without their chief playmaker, Canberra fell apart. They lost their last regular season game to the [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] 32–8, before meekly going out in straight sets in the finals with losses to eventual Grand Finalists the [[St. George Dragons]] and premiers [[Brisbane Broncos]]. In the 1994 season, the Raiders once again finished third in the regular season, and went on to win their third premiership title. In the 1994 Grand Final against Canterbury in which Canberra won 36–12, Boyle, along with [[Jason Death]] were both listed on the four-man interchange bench, but were not used in the game.{{cite book|last=Middleton|first=David|title=Rugby League 1995|page=246|isbn=0-7322-5137-0}} In the 1995 season, Canberra finished second after losing only two matches in the regular season, and once again looked like title contenders. They lost in the preliminary final to Canterbury 25–6. In the 1998 season, his final season at the Canberra club, Boyle had his best season, after playing in 22 of their 26 games, he finished the season with 11 tries second only to [[Mark McLinden]]. Boyle left Canberra at the conclusion of the 1998 season. ===Bradford Bulls=== In 1999, Boyle joined English [[Super League]] side [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]]. After playing 56 games and scoring 17 tries for the Bulls, Boyle decided to retire at the end of the [[2000 Super League season]]. ===Country=== After he retired from Super League, Boyle served as captain-coach of Narooma Devils in [[Group 16 Rugby League|the Group 16 competition]], leading them to the [[Clayton Cup]] in 2003.{{cite web|last=Burnside|first=Bob|title=Narooma Devils bank two big wins in shadows of tribute for Clayton Cup winning team|url=https://www.naroomanewsonline.com.au/story/7357164/devils-bank-two-big-wins-in-shadows-of-tribute-for-clayton-cup-winning-team/|website=Narooma News Online|access-date=November 13, 2023|location=[[Narooma, New South Wales|Narooma]], [[New South Wales]]|date=July 27, 2021}} ==References== {{Reflist|2}} {{Bradford Bulls - 2000 Challenge Cup Final winners}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Boyle, David}} [[Category:1971 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Australian rugby league players]] [[Category:Bradford Bulls players]] [[Category:Canberra Raiders players]] [[Category:Rugby league centres]] [[Category:Rugby league players from Bega, New South Wales]] [[Category:Rugby league wingers]]
